verify friend receives request and have them accept

view transaction records and verify that transfer is logged
select account to transfer between and dollar ammount, then select transfer

testRunSteps.notes: permission to use fingerprint should be set in perferences

take picture of front of check

verify email with statement is received

fields.description:
Goals

<In this section, describe the goals of this test plan.>

Roles and Responsibilities

<In this section, describe the roles involved in this test plan and their responsibilities.>

Participants

Name Role
   

 

Schedule

<In this section, describe the schedule for this testing.>

 

 

Environment(s)

<In this section, describe the environment(s) that will be needed to complete this testing.>
fields.name: Mobile banking

fields.description:
Business requirements: statements of goals, objectives, and outcomes that describe why a change has been initiated. They can apply to the whole of an enterprise, a business area, or a specific initiative.

(IIBA BABOK® Guide v3, section 2.3)
fields.name: Business Requirement

fields.description:
Stakeholder requirements: describe the needs of stakeholders that must be met in order to achieve the business requirements. They may serve as a bridge between business and solution requirements.

(IIBA BABOK® Guide v3, section 2.3)
fields.name: Stakeholder Requirement

fields.description:
Solution requirements: describe the capabilities and qualities of a solution that meets the stakeholder requirements. They provide the appropriate level of detail to allow for the development and implementation of the solution. Solution requirements can be divided into two sub-categories: Functional and Non-Functional (or Quality of Service requirements).

(IIBA BABOK® Guide v3, section 2.3)
fields.name: Solution Requirements

fields.description:
Functional requirements: describe the capabilities that a solution must have in terms of the behavior and information that the solution will manage.

(IIBA BABOK® Guide v3, section 2.3)
fields.name: Functional Requirement

fields.description:
Non-Functional requirements or Quality of Service requirements: do not relate directly to the behavior of functionality of the solution, but rather describe conditions under which a solution must remain effective or qualities that a solution must have.

(IIBA BABOK® Guide v3, section 2.3)
fields.name: Non-Functional Requirement

fields.description:
Transition requirements: describe the capabilities that the solution must have and the conditions the solution must meet to facilitate transition from the current state to the future state, but which are not needed once the change is complete. They are differentiated from other requirements types because they are of a temporary nature. Transition requirements address topics such as data conversion, training, and business continuity.

(IIBA BABOK® Guide v3, section 2.3)
fields.name: Transition Requirement

fields.description:
Business requirements: statements of goals, objectives, and outcomes that describe why a change has been initiated. They can apply to the whole of an enterprise, a business area, or a specific initiative.

(IIBA BABOK® Guide v3, section 2.3)
fields.name: Business Requirements

fields.description: By providing an automated easy to use mobile interface, we can decrease customer traffic at our branch locations. This will help us cut overhead and speed up banking experience
fields.name: Decrease customer traffic in branch locations

fields.description: By automating much of the transaction process (i.e. depositing a check), we can eliminate the cost of human error during a transaction.
fields.name: Cut down on human error

fields.description: by making the process to apply for a loan easier through a mobile application, we hope to increase the percentage of loan applications and speed up the approval process.
fields.name: increase loan applications

fields.description: Through the use of a mobile application, we can continuously update security automatically to stay ahead of ongoing fraud attempts and more easily track fraud when it happens
fields.name: Cut down on fraud

fields.description: By providing state of the art cutting edge technology, we can increase new customer adoption through word of mouth
fields.name: Drive new customer addoption

fields.description: By allowing you to use your mobile wallet we can decrease the cost and fraud possibilities associated with new and stolen credit/debit cards
fields.name: Decrease need for physical credit/debit cards

fields.description: Customer needs the ability to easily transfer funds from an account to another account or person
fields.name: Bank Transfer

fields.description:
Snap a picture of your check and follow on-screen instructions to complete deposit. 

 

This should be available anywhere and anytime.
fields.name: Deposit checks automatically

fields.description: Ability to check balance and transaction history quickly through specific text commands
fields.name: Text Banking

fields.description: User should be able to view and receive paperless statements easily within the application
fields.name: Paperless statements

fields.description: ability to manage and setup direct deposit into your accounts to eliminate a trip to the bank.
fields.name: Setup and manage direct deposit

fields.description: The application should be smart enough to remember the most common actions. This will allow the application to auto complete bank activity to eliminate the potential for human error
fields.name: Remember user actions

fields.description: When account transfers or deposits are made, auto verify will check to make sure all information is correct before submitting the final transfer.
fields.name: Auto verify

fields.description:
When a user logs into the app, if they have set up an online account, import the users preferences automatically

 

note: add preference to allow user to choose weather or not to remember preferences
fields.name: import user preferences

fields.description: Allow user to apply for loans through a 1 click (soft) credit check.
fields.name: 1 touch credit check

fields.description: In app notifications to users off opportunities that they are automatically qualified for. user should be able to apply for opportunities using their finger print acceptance.
fields.name: New opportunity notifications

fields.description: utilize your smart phone's finger print scanning technology to verify the user before gaining access to account information.
fields.name: Biometric Access

fields.description: By allowing automatic updates, we can provide the latest security and react to fraud quickly
fields.name: Automatic updates

fields.description: by allowing the option to enable voice recognition as an extra blanket of security, customers will be able to have more comfort that their information is safe and help to prevent fraud
fields.name: Voice Recognition

fields.description: Link to social media such as Facebook and Twitter to allow user to invite friends to use the app and provide 1 click money transfers between subscribed friends
fields.name: Invite a friend

fields.description:
• Free transfer between banks
  • Point reward system when you pay with the app
  • Free overdraft protection when you use the app
fields.name: Insentives

fields.description: allow your credit/debit cards to be stored electronically in the application that can be used at supporting locations.
fields.name: Electronic wallet

fields.description:
Solution requirements: describe the capabilities and qualities of a solution that meets the stakeholder requirements. They provide the appropriate level of detail to allow for the development and implementation of the solution. Solution requirements can be divided into two sub-categories: Functional and Non-Functional (or Quality of Service requirements).

(IIBA BABOK® Guide v3, section 2.3)
fields.name: Solution Requirements

fields.description:
Functional requirements: describe the capabilities that a solution must have in terms of the behavior and information that the solution will manage.

(IIBA BABOK® Guide v3, section 2.3)
fields.name: Functional Requirements

fields.description: Display a clickable history of all pending/completed/rejected transfers. You should be able to click into each one to view more detailed information
fields.name: View transfer history

fields.description: immediately transfer between existing user accounts by selecting the two accounts, date, and amount. must have verification that entered values are correct.
fields.name: Transfer between account

fields.description: Select a linked friend and a value and automatically send and receive money into linked accounts. This should be a list of linked friends and a dollar value field. Linked friends comes from linking to Facebook and Twitter
fields.name: Transfer between linked friends

fields.description: Option to enable finger print authentication for sending completing a transfer
fields.name: Optional Finger print authentication

fields.description: Need to be able to clearly capture photo of front and back of check.
fields.name: Photo Capture Checks

fields.description: User should receive visual notification on their mobile device once the deposit is successfully completed
fields.name: Notification of completion

fields.description:
Need setup window to enter users phone number and check box to agree to terms and conditions. 

Need field to enter authorization code that will be sent to users entered phone number. 

Once authorization code is entered, validation window will pop up with preferences for what the customer wants to get notified on:

  1. Deposits
  2. Withdrawals
  3. Low Balance
  4. Transferes
fields.name: Text banking setup window

fields.description:
In the preferences window, customer should have access to choosing which statements they want to receive through email:

  • Receive automatic email notifications when your Online Statements are available.
  • Online Statements are saved as PDFs, which you can access, save, and print as needed.
  • Online Statements are available for many accounts, including: checking, savings, credit cards, home mortgages, personal loans and lines of credit, home equity loans and lines of credit, student loans, brokerage, and trust and managed investments.
fields.name: Paperless Statement Preferences

fields.description: If the mobile device being used has biometric sensors, and if biometric has been set up, allow user to press finger to scanner at login screen and automatically be logged into application.
fields.name: Auto log in with biometric scan when available

fields.description: provide preferences in setting menu for user to turn off and turn on biometric access. Allow the user to set how many minutes after a scan the app will stay logged in for.
fields.name: Biometric Login Preferences

fields.description: Once an automatic update is complete, give banner notification to user with link to release notes.
fields.name: Update notification

fields.description: need options in the preferences screen to allow user to enable voice recognition commands
fields.name: Voice Recognition Menu Options

fields.description:
The following voice commands should be supported if the user has enabled voice recognition in the app:

 

  1. "Show me my balance"
  2. "Show me my last statement"
  3. "Transfer funds"
  4. "Show withdrawal history"
  5. "Show my last deposit"
  6. "Pay a bill"
fields.name: Support voice commands

fields.description:
Non-Functional requirements or Quality of Service requirements: do not relate directly to the behavior of functionality of the solution, but rather describe conditions under which a solution must remain effective or qualities that a solution must have.

(IIBA BABOK® Guide v3, section 2.3)
fields.name: Non-Functional Requirements
Security

fields.description: enforce that the application will log out a customer after the user set time period has passed after a fingerprint scan
fields.name: Enforce user specified logout after fingerprint scan

fields.description: utilize the native biometric scan capabilities with supported mobile devices to authenticate behind the scenes.
fields.name: work with mobile phone native scanning

fields.description: Need to be able to authenticate users phone number that will be used for text banking. This should work by sending a security code to the phone number entered that when entered in the mobile app, will authenticate and validate the users phone number and send out a validation text to start the process.
fields.name: Authenticate phone number

fields.description: Passwords must consist of a minimum of 8 characters and containing at least 1 non-alphabet character
fields.name: Strong password requirements

fields.description: Access permissions for application data may only be changed by the system's data administrator
fields.name: Access permissions
Audit

fields.description: System must maintain full traceability of transactions
fields.name: Transaction traces
Capacity

fields.description: Must support concurrent transfers for shared accounts. With shared accounts, users may be executing transferssimultaneously.
fields.name: Concurrent Transfers
Performance

fields.description: Response times – application loading, browser refresh times, etc.
fields.name: Response Times

fields.description: Processing times – functions, calculations, imports, exports
fields.name: Processing times
Availability

fields.description: Operational hours should be 24x7 with no downtime. Planned downtime must be mitigated through redundancy.
fields.name: Hours of operation
Reliability

fields.description: Mean Time Between Failures – What is the acceptablethreshold for down-time?
fields.name: MTBF
Recoverability

fields.description: Backup frequencies – how often is the transaction data, config data, code backed-up?
fields.name: Backup frequencies
Robustness

fields.description: Fault trapping (I/O) , Application Hooks, SMNP – how to handle failures ?
fields.name: Fault trapping
Maintainability

fields.description: Conformance to Enterprise Architecture standards
fields.name: Conformance
Usability

fields.description: Utilize the open source CMU Sphinx speech recognition toolkit for voice recognition.
fields.name: CMU Sphinx library
Documentation

fields.description:
Transition requirements: describe the capabilities that the solution must have and the conditions the solution must meet to facilitate transition from the current state to the future state, but which are not needed once the change is complete. They are differentiated from other requirements types because they are of a temporary nature. Transition requirements address topics such as data conversion, training, and business continuity.

(IIBA BABOK® Guide v3, section 2.3)

fields.name: request for transfer not received by linked friend

fields.description:
Purpose

 

Item tracking is used to capture and assign responsibility for issues and stakeholder concerns that pose an impact to the solution.

 

Description

 

Item tracking is an organized approach used by business analysts to address stakeholder concerns. Stakeholders may identify such item types as actions, assumptions, constraints, dependencies, defects, enhancements, and issues.

 

When a stakeholder concern is first raised, it is assessed to determine if it is viable. If viable, the concern is classified as a specific item type so that it can be better tracked and controlled by a process that works towards the item’s closure. During its life cycle, an item is assigned to one or more stakeholders who are responsible for its resolution.

 

Item tracking tracks the item from the initial recording of the concern and its degree of impact to its agreed-upon closure. The item tracking record may be shared with stakeholders to ensure transparency and visibility into the status and progress of items in the record.
fields.name: Item Tracking

fields.description:
A group or individual with a relationship to the change, the need, or the solution.

Stakeholders are often defined in terms of interest in, impact on, and influence over the change. Stakeholders are grouped based on their relationship to the needs, changes, and solutions. 

(IIBA BABOK v3, table 2.1.1)
fields.name: Stakeholders
